verses.text: A lamentation: 'the virgin of Israel is fallen'; the thousand shall leave but a hundred. Mourning foretold. The prophet weeps over a ruin not yet come.
A lamentation: 'the virgin of Israel is fallen'; the thousand shall leave but a hundred. Mourning foretold. The prophet weeps over a ruin not yet come.
A lamentation: 'the virgin of Israel is fallen'; the thousand shall leave but a hundred. Mourning foretold. The prophet weeps over a ruin not yet come.
'Seek ye me, and ye shall live'; seek not Bethel's idols, but the LORD, lest He break out like fire. Life in seeking. The way to live is to seek God Himself, not false shrines.
'Seek ye me, and ye shall live'; seek not Bethel's idols, but the LORD, lest He break out like fire. Life in seeking. The way to live is to seek God Himself, not false shrines.
'Seek ye me, and ye shall live'; seek not Bethel's idols, but the LORD, lest He break out like fire. Life in seeking. The way to live is to seek God Himself, not false shrines.
Ye turn judgment to wormwood; seek Him who made 'the seven stars and Orion,' who 'turneth the shadow of death into the morning.' The Maker pleads. The God of the stars calls for justice on earth.
Ye turn judgment to wormwood; seek Him who made 'the seven stars and Orion,' who 'turneth the shadow of death into the morning.' The Maker pleads. The God of the stars calls for justice on earth.
Ye turn judgment to wormwood; seek Him who made 'the seven stars and Orion,' who 'turneth the shadow of death into the morning.' The Maker pleads. The God of the stars calls for justice on earth.

They hate the reprover and tread on the poor; they build houses they shall not dwell in; 'the prudent shall keep silence.' Injustice rewarded with loss. Wealth wrung from the poor will not be enjoyed.

They hate the reprover and tread on the poor; they build houses they shall not dwell in; 'the prudent shall keep silence.' Injustice rewarded with loss. Wealth wrung from the poor will not be enjoyed.
'Seek good, and not evil, that ye may live'; hate evil, love good, 'and establish judgment in the gate.' The plain way. True religion shows in justice and a love of good.
'Seek good, and not evil, that ye may live'; hate evil, love good, 'and establish judgment in the gate.' The plain way. True religion shows in justice and a love of good.
Wailing shall fill the streets and vineyards, for 'I will pass through thee,' saith the LORD. The Judge passes. As in Egypt of old, His passing brings mourning to the guilty.
Wailing shall fill the streets and vineyards, for 'I will pass through thee,' saith the LORD. The Judge passes. As in Egypt of old, His passing brings mourning to the guilty.
Woe to those who long for the day of the LORD; 'it is darkness, and not light,' like fleeing a lion to meet a bear. False hope. The day they crave will bring terror, not relief.
Woe to those who long for the day of the LORD; 'it is darkness, and not light,' like fleeing a lion to meet a bear. False hope. The day they crave will bring terror, not relief.
Woe to those who long for the day of the LORD; 'it is darkness, and not light,' like fleeing a lion to meet a bear. False hope. The day they crave will bring terror, not relief.
'I hate, I despise your feast days'; God will not accept their offerings; 'take... away from me the noise of thy songs.' Worship rejected. Ritual without righteousness is loathsome to God.
'I hate, I despise your feast days'; God will not accept their offerings; 'take... away from me the noise of thy songs.' Worship rejected. Ritual without righteousness is loathsome to God.
'I hate, I despise your feast days'; God will not accept their offerings; 'take... away from me the noise of thy songs.' Worship rejected. Ritual without righteousness is loathsome to God.

'But let judgment run down as waters, and righteousness as a mighty stream.' They carried idols and shall go captive beyond Damascus. The true offering. God wants justice flowing freely, not empty sacrifice.
